preview_text:
The model favors Croatia at 47%, while Panama's win probability stands at 23%. The underdog's chances are meaningful but well below the favorite, reflecting a substantial quality gap in the forecasting framework.

The 30% draw probability indicates a competitive match with genuine uncertainty around the outcome. The spread between Croatia's 47% and Panama's 23% suggests the model expects the favorite to control possession and chances, yet the draw sitting at three-in-ten reflects the possibility of a tight contest that neither side decisively wins.

Model and market diverge notably. The sportsbook consensus rates Croatia 15 percentage points higher (62%) than the model (47%), while assigning Panama roughly 9 points lower (15% versus 23%). Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard presents both; it does not claim knowledge of which assessment is correct, only that material disagreement exists between the statistical forecast and consensus betting.
